dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7999 IH: Breakfast After the Bell Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Bera introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Education and Workforce

form.official-title: To amend the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act to provide additional reimbursement to schools with a breakfast after the bell program, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Breakfast After the Bell Act of 2026.

section.header: Increased reimbursement for breakfast after the bell
section.text: Section 11(a) of the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act (42 U.S.C. 1759a(a)) is amended by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.text: A State educational agency shall receive an additional reimbursement of 10 cents for each reimbursable breakfast served—

clause.text: after the beginning of a school day;
through a breakfast after the bell program; and
during a school year by a school that—

subclause.text: is served by a local educational agency that elects to receive special assistance payments under paragraph (1)(F)(ii) for such school for such year; or
had a percentage of enrolled students who were identified students that was not less than 40 percent during the prior school year.

subparagraph.text: A State educational agency that receives an additional reimbursement for a breakfast under subparagraph (A) shall disburse such additional reimbursement to the school that served such breakfast.
In this paragraph, the term breakfast after the bell program means a school breakfast program that—

clause.text: serves breakfast to students after the start of the school day; and

uses a service model, including service of breakfast in the classroom or at a kiosk outside the cafeteria of the school, that the State educational agency determines increases participation in the breakfast program.
